text.title: devops-platform-golden-paths Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for devops-platform-golden-paths as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Building golden paths without developer input, then wondering why adoption stays below 20%.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Golden paths that solve the wrong problems are invisible to developers. Interview developers about their top friction points BEFORE building any template. The top-3 friction points become the first 3 golden paths.

failure-mode.description: Making golden paths mandatory (blocking deviation via policy enforcement).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mandatory golden paths recreate the operations ticket queue. Developers route around mandatory paths through shadow infrastructure or "exceptions" that grow into permanent workarounds. Opinionated-but-optional with documented escape hatches drives 89% voluntary adoption (Spotify).

failure-mode.description: Building 15 golden paths before validating the first one.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Over-engineering before validating value is the primary cause of platform abandonment. Build one golden path, measure adoption, iterate. Spotify took 6 months between catalog launch and the first scaffolder template.
